meaning: n.[动](昆虫的)侧单眼,家系,家谱
examples: If surviving copies are few, the stemma perforce brings into proximity manuscripts that were widely separated in time and place of origin.
Conversely, the stemma can also bestow a semblance of separation on manuscripts written within a few months of one another or even in the same room.
The foundation of new forms of pedigree of clans in 15th century extended the understanding of inner and outer stemma.
